title: Trust Building, East Adams Comparison, Community Ambassadors, HFA Priority Designation, and Next Steps
summary: Council delivers closing message: city has 'failed that corridor for a long time' with 'decades and decades' of broken housing promises; transparency and follow-through non-negotiable. Developers show completed Section 8 project in downtown Buffalo as quality example; council acknowledges construction quality but emphasizes process over product -- 'we're moving people... we're moving bodies across the street... I am not in favor of warehousing people.' Developers completed community needs assessment (30% response rate); implementing homework help with Boys and Girls Club on-site based on survey feedback. Commissioner Collins notes community ambassador programs should be utilized; East Adams/Pioneer Homes experienced same dual-community dynamic where demolished-section residents did not identify with Pioneer Homes community. CNY Jazz held first-ever summer series at Parkside, facilitated by city. HFA (Housing Finance Agency) designates Parkside as '#1 priority in Syracuse' -- New York State committed to investment. Collins holds weekly meetings with development team and weekly HCR calls. Bilingual engagement planned (English and Spanish). Next community meeting: February 10 at Parkside community room (capacity ~50-60). Current maintenance staff will not perform renovations (separate contractors). Committee chair invites developers to return regularly for progress updates.

speakers.name: Michael Collins
speakers.role: Commissioner for Neighborhood and Business Development

political_signal.description: Council expressing deep institutional skepticism while acknowledging developer progress, demanding accountability standard set by East Adams process

processing_notes: Fire, DPD, Finance in detected_departments are contextual (fire marshal reference, permits, tax discussion); actual department is Neighborhood and Business Development
CNY Jazz = Central New York Jazz Arts Foundation; Boys and Girls Club = on-site community partner
HFA = Housing Finance Agency (subdivision of HCR); '#1 priority in Syracuse' = significant state funding signal
February 10 community meeting = engagement milestone to track; committee chair addressed as 'Jimmy' in transcript
